dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6387 IH: Fire Improvement and Reforming Exceptional Events Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Evans of Colorado introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce

form.official-title: To amend the Clean Air Act to require revisions to regulations governing the review and handling of air quality monitoring data influenced by exceptional events or actions to mitigate wildfire risk.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Fire Improvement and Reforming Exceptional Events Act or the FIRE Act.

section.header: Air quality monitoring data influenced by exceptional events or actions to mitigate wildfire risk
section.text: Section 319(b) of the Clean Air Act (42 U.S.C. 7619(b)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in the subsection heading, by inserting Or actions To mitigate wildfire risk after Events;

subparagraph.text: in the paragraph heading, by striking Definition of exceptional event and inserting Definitions;
in subparagraph (A), by redesignating clauses (i) through (iv), as subclauses (I) through (IV), respectively;
by striking (A) and all that follows through an event that— and inserting the following:

clause.text: The term exceptional event means an event that—

subparagraph.text: by amending subclause (III) of subparagraph (A)(i), as redesignated, to read as follows:

item.text: caused by a human activity that is intended to mirror the occurrence or reoccurrence of a natural event; or
caused by a human activity that is unlikely to recur; and

subparagraph.text: by striking subparagraph (B) and inserting the following:

clause.text: In this subsection, the term exceptional event does not include—

subclause.text: ordinarily occurring stagnation of air masses;
air pollution relating to source noncompliance.

subparagraph.text: by adding at the end the following:
The term action to mitigate wildfire risk means a prescribed fire or similar measure, undertaken in accordance with State approved practices, to reduce the risk and severity of wildfires.

clause.text: by striking March 1, 2006 and inserting 18 months after the date of enactment of the FIRE Act;
by inserting revisions to before regulations; and
by adding or actions to mitigate wildfire risk before the period at the end;
by inserting including proposed revisions to regulations, after subparagraph (A),;
by inserting or action to mitigate wildfire risk after an exceptional event; and
by striking paragraph (3) and inserting this section; and

subparagraph.text: by adding at the end the following:

When more than one State notifies the Administrator of its intent to submit a petition pursuant to this section for an exceptional event or an action to mitigate wildfire risk for the same air quality event, or, if the Administrator determines such a multistate air quality event has occurred, the Administrator shall conduct regional modeling and analysis sufficient to satisfy the demonstration required for an exceptional event or an action to mitigate wildfire risk petition for such air quality event.

Not later than 12 months after the date of enactment of the FIRE Act, the Administrator shall establish, and thereafter update monthly, a public website describing the status of all petitions submitted pursuant to this section for exceptional events and actions to mitigate wildfire risk.
by redesignating clauses (ii) through (v) as clauses (iii) through (vi), respectively; and
by inserting after clause (i) the following:

clause.text: the principle that actions to mitigate wildfire risk can play an important role in reducing the magnitude and frequency of wildfires;

subparagraph.text: in clause (i), by inserting or action to mitigate wildfire risk before must be;
by amending clause (ii) to read as follows:

clause.text: a clear causal relationship must exist, or be reasonably expected to exist, between the measured exceedances of a national ambient air quality standard and the exceptional event or action to mitigate wildfire risk to demonstrate that the exceptional event or action to mitigate wildfire risk caused a specific air pollution concentration at a particular air quality monitoring location;

subparagraph.text: by amending clause (iv) to read as follows:

clause.text: there are criteria and procedures for the Governor of a State to petition the Administrator to exclude air quality monitoring data that is directly due to exceptional events or actions to mitigate wildfire risk from use in determinations by the Administrator with respect to—

subclause.text: area or source exceedances or violations of the national ambient air quality standards;
the designation, redesignation, classification, or reclassification of an area;
the demonstration by a State of attainment of a national ambient air quality standard;
finding a State implementation plan to be inadequate; or
